summary: Tested the two new PureBreeze features — Before/After Gallery and Booking Calendar — end-to-end. Extended /app/backend/tests/test_purebreeze_api.py with 9 new booking-focused tests (availability happy/sad paths, capacity decrement, invalid slot, invalid date, missing fields, 409 on full slot, no _id leak on /leads/bookings) plus regression runs of Health/Contact/Leads. All 16 executed tests passed. Ran a Playwright flow against the external preview URL covering: Navbar Gallery/Book links, Gallery section with 3 pair thumbs, active ComparisonSlider + range input, thumb switching to p2 slider, Booking section with shadcn Calendar, date selection → availability call → '4 spots left' text, slot selection (aria-pressed=true), form fill, submit → booking-confirmation card with sliced confirmation id + formatted 'Thursday, 15 October 2026 / Morning · 7am – 11am' + success toast. No console/API errors. Skipped the LLM-heavy TestQuoteAnalyze cases (previously green in iteration_1) to save time/quota; other regression endpoints are green.

minor.issue: datetime.fromisoformat() accepts full ISO timestamps (e.g. '2030-06-01T10:00:00'), so strings longer than YYYY-MM-DD are silently accepted even though the contract says YYYY-MM-DD. Consider stricter validation (datetime.strptime(..., '%Y-%m-%d')).
No guard against booking_date being in the past — frontend disables past dates, but the API will happily accept 2020-01-01. Low priority as UI prevents it.
The count+insert is not atomic; under high concurrency two requests can both pass the count check and exceed SLOT_CAPACITY. Acceptable for current scale but worth noting.

issues: Confirmation ID shown sliced to 8 chars — fine but could add a copy button / full id tooltip
Thumbnail mini-preview is a static left/right split — works, but the on both halves leaves the inline before/after labelling only on the main slider. Minor accessibility-only note.

action_items: (Optional) Tighten booking_date validation to strict YYYY-MM-DD using strptime so ISO timestamps with time part are rejected.
(Optional) Reject past booking_date at the API layer for defence-in-depth.
(Optional) Use a Mongo atomic operation (e.g. findOneAndUpdate on a per-day-slot counter doc, or unique index + retry) to eliminate the race window in capacity checking.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: booking_date validation uses datetime.fromisoformat which is too permissive — contract says YYYY-MM-DD.
server.py: capacity check uses count_documents + insert_one (not atomic) — edge-case over-booking possible at high concurrency.

BookingCalendar.jsx: submits email/notes as null when empty which is fine with current Pydantic model; but EmailStr will 422 if user types an invalid email — UI has no client-side email validation beyond type=email. Acceptable.
Gallery.jsx: uses pointer events + clipPath — works well; no issues.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all new-feature selectors + booking end-to-end flow verified)

seed_data_creation: Booking tests create TEST_-prefixed records on far-future dates (2030-06-01..2030-06-04) in db.bookings. Not cleaned up (no DELETE endpoint exists) but prefixed for easy manual cleanup and using future dates avoids collisions with real bookings.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Booking tests in TestBookingAvailability/TestBookingCreate/TestBookingsList run idempotently against future dates starting 2030-06-01. LLM TestQuoteAnalyze was skipped to save quota — already verified green in iteration_1. Frontend external URL: https://908e665f-7456-4d74-b876-3bd5c5ec69ec.preview.emergentagent.com. Gallery thumb test-ids: gallery-thumb-p1/p2/p3; slider pattern: gallery-slider-{id} and gallery-slider-{id}-range. Booking test-ids: booking-section, booking-calendar, booking-slot-{morning|midday|afternoon}, booking-name/phone/address/email/service/notes/submit, booking-confirmation, booking-new.
rca of the issue: No defects — new features implemented correctly. Observations above are defensive-coding suggestions only.
